meta.title: DNA-Driven Development
meta.description: Code is an artifact of DNA, not the other way around. Stop prompting. Start compiling.

props.title: Code Is an Artifact of DNA
props.subtitle: Not the other way around.
props.meta: Leviathan Project · April 2026 · The Constraint Engineering Series

props.content:
Markdown describes. Agents need prescriptions, not descriptions. YAML prescribes—behaviors, conditions, failure modes, constraints—executable structure that compiles into runtime behavior.

You're not telling an agent what the system does. You're telling it what the system IS.

Proved: 14/15 treatment vs 0/15 control across Claude + Gemini. DNA YAML steers agent behavior. Prose does not.

props.label: THE THESIS
props.title: Stop Prompting. Start Compiling.
props.subtitle: DNA is the behavioral contract layer. Not a file format—a concept.

props.content: DNA is NOT a file format. It is a concept. The .dna.yaml extension is a hint for humans, not a parser requirement. DNA sections—constraints, behaviors, failure_modes—can live IN config.yaml, in .flow.yaml, anywhere. The compiler takes a FeatureDna object, not a file extension.

A constraint is a flowmind definition. A harness is the tooling to run it. Leviathan is a meta-harness—it defines and runs its own constraint harnesses.

90% of cases: Lev IS the harness. Define the DNA, write the flowmind, Lev executes and validates. 10% of cases: custom harness needed (AppTestr for iOS, NAAC for ATC). But you STILL define the DNA first. The DNA cascade builds code around the behavioral contract.

This is not theory. The compilation pipeline is built. The intent compiler exists at `core/flowmind/src/intent-compiler/`. The target compilers emit to 8+ framework formats. The eval triad validates structural, behavioral, and cross-framework correctness.

props.label: THE REPRESENTATION TRIANGLE
props.title: Three Layers. One Compilation Pipeline.
props.subtitle: Every feature has three representations. They are a compilation pipeline, not three independent documents.

props.title: Markdown = Intent
props.content:
**Question**: What does the human want?
**Consumers**: Humans, intent compiler
**Shearing layer**: Stuff (daily—intent changes frequently)
**Compilation**: Ambiguous NL → gated (Gate 0 regex, Gate 1 LLM disambiguation)

Example: "I want a button that can only be red/green and when I click and I'm mad tell JP in Slack."

props.title: DNA YAML = Behavioral Contract
props.content:
**Question**: What IS this thing? Behaviors, constraints, failure modes, conditions.
**Consumers**: Agents, QA agents, gates, compilers, tribunal, validator chain
**Shearing layer**: Site (decades—behavioral truth changes rarely)
**Compilation**: Canonical YAML → deterministic structural transform (no gates needed)

Example: `colors_allowed: [red, green]`, `failure_modes: [invalid_color, sentiment_negative_click]`

props.title: FlowMind = Execution Contract
props.content:
**Question**: How do you DO it? Triggers, actions, topology, wiring.
**Consumers**: FlowMind, lev exec, orchestration, execution contract
**Shearing layer**: Structure (30-50 years—execution contracts are load-bearing)
**Compilation**: GraphDocument → ecStart()/ecNext() state machine → adapter dispatch

Example: `trigger: click + sentiment.negative → action: slack.send(channel: jp)`

props.label: THE COMPILATION PIPELINE
props.title: Human Speaks → Code Ships
props.subtitle: Four forward steps. Two reverse steps. One feedback loop.

props.title: Human Intent
props.content:
Natural language → *.md
Compiler: human

props.title: Intent Compiler
props.content:
*.md → *.dna.yaml
Gated: regex + LLM disambiguation

props.title: DNA Compiler
props.content:
*.dna.yaml → *.flow.yaml
Deterministic structural transform

props.title: Execution
props.content:
*.flow.yaml → runtime artifacts
lev exec --flow=<path>

props.title: Decompiler
props.content:
*.flow.yaml → *.dna.yaml
For audit, debugging, review

props.title: NLU Decompiler
props.content:
*.dna.yaml → *.md
Human-readable rendering

props.title: The Feedback Loop
props.content:
**Trigger**: Regression found in runtime.
**Action**: Update *.dna.yaml → recompile *.flow.yaml → regenerate tests.
**Rule**: Never fix code directly. Fix DNA. Code follows.

props.label: THE EVIDENCE
props.title: 14/15 Treatment vs 0/15 Control
props.subtitle: DNA YAML steers agent behavior. Prose does not. This is not opinion—it's measured.

props.content:
We ran the experiment on 2026-03-22. Same task, same models (Claude + Gemini), two conditions:

**Treatment**: Agent receives DNA YAML with constraints, behaviors, and failure modes. 14 out of 15 runs produced correct, constraint-compliant output.

**Control**: Agent receives prose markdown with the same information described in natural language. 0 out of 15 runs produced fully constraint-compliant output.

The model is the same. The information is the same. The format changes everything. Prompts converge, harnesses differentiate. DNA YAML is the harness.

props.label: EVERYTHING COMPILES
props.title: These Concepts Are No Longer Hand-Authored
props.subtitle: Prompts, skills, agents, chains, workflows, RAG, tool use—all compilation artifacts from DNA.

props.title: Prompts
props.content: A node field in a FlowMind graph. The system-prompt target compiler generates them from DNA. You don't write prompts—you compile them.

props.title: Skills
props.content: A FlowMind document with trigger conditions. No separate skill format. A skill IS a reactive graph.

props.title: Agents
props.content: A FlowMind node type. 50 flowminds don't need 50 agents—one runtime walks all graphs.

props.title: Chains
props.content: Linear FlowMind topology (node.next). The degenerate case of a graph.

props.title: Workflows
props.content: FlowMind graphs with typed nodes (action, gate, loop, subgraph, parallel).

props.title: RAG
props.content: A LOAD node with a search fusion step. Context assembly is a target compiler, not a separate system.

props.title: Tool Use
props.content: A function node type with op/action fields. The tool registry is a node in the graph.

props.label: FRAMEWORK PARASITISM
props.title: DNA Compiles to Any Framework
props.subtitle: Like LLVM targeting x86/ARM/WASM. Each new target = ~100-200 LOC.

props.content:
DNA is framework-agnostic. The behavioral contract is the source of truth. Target compilers project it into any framework's native format. Today it compiles to FlowMind graphs and reactive hooks. Tomorrow it could compile to CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, or anything else.

The constraints are framework-agnostic. The runtime is a projection. This means you can switch frameworks without rewriting your contracts. Your DNA survives the framework churn.

props.label: WHY THIS WORKS
props.title: The Fractal Property
props.subtitle: Every feature has its own DNA. Every module has its own DNA. They compose.

props.content: QA agents validate against DNA. Regressions update DNA. Code is regenerated. The human stays on intent. The machine handles the rest. This is not configuration. This is compilation.

props.title: Structural
props.content:
Does the DNA compile? Does output match the golden .flow.yaml?
Speed: milliseconds. Gate: CI-blocking.

props.title: Behavioral
props.content:
Does the compiled graph execute correctly through ecStart/ecNext?
Speed: seconds. Gate: CI-blocking.

props.title: Cross-Framework
props.content:
Do all target exports produce semantically equivalent output?
Targets: LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI, GitAgent, IntentC, Google ADK, Mastra.
Speed: minutes. Gate: scheduled.

props.label: THE EVOLUTION
props.title: Context → Harness → Constraint
props.subtitle: Three eras. Most teams are still in era one.

props.content:
Context engineering (2024): Craft the perfect prompt, stuff the context window, hope. Harness engineering (2025): Build eval loops, add quality gates, measure before shipping. Constraint engineering (2026): Define what the system IS, not what it should do. DNA contracts compile into harnesses, which compile into runtime behavior.

The constraints are the product. Code is the downstream artifact.

props.title: 2024: Prompting
props.content: Craft prompts. Hope they work. Iterate manually. The BASIC era.

props.title: 2025: Harnesses
props.content: Build eval loops. Add gates. Measure. Prompts converge, harnesses differentiate.

props.title: 2026: DNA Compilation
props.content: Define what the system IS. DNA compiles to harnesses. Harnesses compile to runtime. Code is an artifact.

props.title: ← Part 1: Constraints Are the Product
props.content: The philosophy. Why constraint-first engineering is the only approach that scales. The Genesis Pattern, the eval flow, the adversarial moat.

props.title: ← Leviathan Home
props.content: The Universal Agent Runtime. One runtime, every agent, every surface.
